text.title: microservices-saga-pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for microservices-saga-pattern. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every saga step MUST have a paired compensation transaction. A saga without compensation for every completed step is incomplete and MUST be rejected in code review.
rule.rationale: Half-compensated sagas leave business entities in inconsistent states. Inventory reserved but payment refunded with no order cancellation means the item is unavailable to other buyers indefinitely.

rule.statement: Compensation transactions MUST be idempotent. The orchestrator may retry compensation multiple times; duplicate executions must be safe.
rule.rationale: Networks fail; orchestrators crash mid-compensation. Idempotency ensures that retrying compensation does not double-refund, double-release, or double-cancel.

rule.statement: Saga state MUST be persisted and observable. Each state transition (STARTED, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, COMPENSATING, FAILED) must be logged with the saga ID, step name, and timestamp.
rule.rationale: Without observable state, debugging a half-compensated saga requires forensic log analysis across multiple services. A saga table or workflow engine log is the audit trail.
